revision.text:
{{Basic Information on Samurai|Name=Suwa Yorishige|Era=Late Kamakura period - Nanbokucho period|Birth=Not Clear|Death=August 19, 2nd year of Kenmu ( September 6, 1335 )|Alias ​​=Moritaka? [2] , Shoun Nyudo|Rank=Junior Fifth Rank [2] , Governor of Mikawa [1]|Lord=Hojo Clan|Clan=Suwa|Parents=Father: Suwa Morishige ?, Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo)?, Suwa Nobushige ? [1]|Siblings=Shigenao , Yorinari , Atsutada , Yorimori , Akishige , or Tsunemitsu ? [ 1 ]|child=Tokitsugu , Takashige , Takatsugu , Banzai [1]|Rename=Moritsugu [1] →Yorishige →Shoun (Dharma name)}}
'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[military commander]] from the late [[Kamakura period]] to the [[Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to be the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa Taisha]], but the details are unknown. There are various theories about his father.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the Suwa clan was the retainer of the Hojo clan, the shugo (guardian) of Shinano Province for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan came into conflict with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new Shugo of Shinano by the Kenmu Restoration, and due to dissatisfaction with the rule of the Ogasawara clan, Yorishige and Suwa Tokitsugu, father and son, took on the surviving son of Regent Hojo Takatoki, Hojo Tokiyuki, and started the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo, and eventually forced Ashikaga Tadayoshi to flee, temporarily occupying Kamakura, but was defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, who had been sent from Kyoto, and Yorishige committed suicide at Shochoju-in with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>.
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and take him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of Daishugi) was inherited by his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige.png|A memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture; the top of the tower shows signs of repair.) File:Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up).jpg|Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up) File:Suwa Shrine Maemiya Worship Site.jpg|Suwa Shrine Front Shrine Worship Site (The memorial tower is on the left as you descend) </gallery>
== Footnotes == <references/>
== Related Works == * [[Matsui Yusei]] "[[The Elusive Samurai]]" (serialized in [[Weekly Shonen Jump]])
== Source == * "Umematsuron" * "Taiheiki" (Volume 13, The Death of Tokiyuki in the Case of the Ashikaga Lord's Departure to the Eastern Provinces)

'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[military commander]] from the late [[Kamakura period]] to the [[Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to be the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa Taisha]], but the details are unknown. There are various theories about his father.

With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo, and eventually forced Ashikaga Tadayoshi to flee, temporarily occupying Kamakura, but was defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, who had been sent from Kyoto, and Yorishige committed suicide at Shochoju-in with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>.
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and take him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of Daishugi) was inherited by his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).

{{Infobox samurai | Name = Suwa Yorishige | Era = Late Kamakura period - Nanboku-chō period | Birth = Unknown | Death = August 19, 1335 (2nd year of Kenmu) | Alias = Moritaka, Shoun Nyudo | Rank = Junior Fifth Rank, Governor of Mikawa | Lord = Hōjō Clan | Clan = Suwa | Parents = Father: Suwa Morishige, Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo), Suwa Nobushige | Siblings = Shigenao, Yorinari, Atsutada, Yorimori, Akishige, Tsunemitsu | Children = Tokitsugu, Takashige, Takatsugu, Banzai | Rename = Moritsugu → Yorishige → Shoun (Dharma name) }}
'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[military commander]] from the late [[Kamakura period]] to the [[Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief priest]] of [[Suwa Taisha]], but the details are unknown. There are various theories about his father.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the Suwa clan was a retainer of the Hōjō clan, who served as the shugo (provincial governor) of Shinano Province for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan came into conflict with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new shugo of Shinano by the Kenmu Restoration. Due to dissatisfaction with the rule of the Ogasawara clan, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu allied with the surviving son of Regent Hōjō Takatoki, Hōjō Tokiyuki, and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu, and Koyama Hidetomo. Eventually, they forced Ashikaga Tadayoshi to flee, temporarily occupying Kamakura. However, they were defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in alongside his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hōjō Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of Daishugi) was inherited by his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige.png|A memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture; the top of the tower shows signs of repair.) File:Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up).jpg|Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up) File:Suwa Shrine Maemiya Worship Site.jpg|Suwa Shrine Front Shrine Worship Site (The memorial tower is on the left as you descend) </gallery>
== Footnotes == <references/>

== Sources == * "Umematsuron" * "Taiheiki" (Volume 13, The Death of Tokiyuki in the Case of the Ashikaga Lord's Departure to the Eastern Provinces)

'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[military commander]] active from the late [[Kamakura period]] to the [[Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the Suwa clan served as retainers to the Hojo clan, the ''shugo'' (governor) of Shinano Province for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the Kenmu Restoration. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported Hojo Tokiyuki, the surviving son of Regent Hojo Takatoki, and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced Ashikaga Tadayoshi to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).

[2], Shoun Nyudo | rank = Junior Fifth Rank [2], Governor of Mikawa [1] | lord = Hojo Clan | clan = Suwa | parents = Father: Suwa Morishige? / Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo)? / Suwa Nobushige? [1] | siblings = Shigenao, Yorinari, Atsutada, Yorimori, Akishige, or Tsunemitsu? [1] | children = Tokitsugu, Takashige, Takatsugu, Banzai [1] | rename = Moritsugu [1] → Yorishige → Shoun (Dharma name) }}
'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[military commander]] active from the late [[Kamakura period]] to the [[Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).

{{Short description|Military commander}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Suwa Yorishige | era = Late Kamakura period - Nanbokucho period | birth = Unknown | death = August 19, 1335 (Kenmu 2) | alias = Moritaka? [2], Shoun Nyudo | rank = Junior Fifth Rank [2], Governor of Mikawa [1] | lord = Hojo Clan | clan = Suwa | parents = Father: Suwa Morishige? / Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo)? / Suwa Nobushige? [1] | siblings = Shigenao, Yorinari, Atsutada, Yorimori, Akishige, or Tsunemitsu? [1] | children = Tokitsugu, Takashige, Takatsugu, Banzai [1] | rename = Moritsugu [1] → Yorishige → Shoun (Dharma name) }}
'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[military commander]] active from the late [[Kamakura period]] to the [[Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige.png|A memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige (Miyagawa, [[Chino, Nagano|Chino City, Nagano Prefecture]]; the top of the tower shows signs of repair.) File:Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up).jpg|Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up) File:Suwa Shrine Maemiya Worship Site.jpg|Suwa Shrine Front Shrine Worship Site (The memorial tower is on the left as you descend) </gallery>
== Footnotes == <references/>
== Related works == * [[Matsui Yusei]] "[[The Elusive Samurai]]" (serialized in [[Weekly Shonen Jump|''Weekly Shonen Jump'']])
== Sources == * "Umematsuron" * "Taiheiki" (Volume 13, The Death of Tokiyuki in the Case of the Ashikaga Lord's Departure to the Eastern Provinces)

[2], Shoun Nyudo | rank = Junior Fifth Rank [2], Governor of Mikawa [1] | lord = Hojo Clan | clan = Suwa | parents = Father: Suwa Morishige? / Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo)? / Suwa Nobushige? [1] | siblings = Shigenao, Yorinari, Atsutada, Yorimori, Akishige, or Tsunemitsu? [1] | children = Tokitsugu, Takashige, Takatsugu, Banzai [1] | rename = Moritsugu [1] → Yorishige → Shoun (Dharma name) }}
'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[military commander]] active from the late [[Kamakura period]] to the [[Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige.png|A memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige (Miyagawa, [[Chino, Nagano|Chino City, Nagano Prefecture]]; the top of the tower shows signs of repair.) File:Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up).jpg|Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up) File:Suwa Shrine Maemiya Worship Site.jpg|Suwa Shrine Front Shrine Worship Site (The memorial tower is on the left as you descend) </gallery>

[2], Shoun Nyudo | rank = Junior Fifth Rank [2], Governor of Mikawa [1] | lord = Hojo Clan | clan = Suwa | parents = Father: Suwa Morishige? / Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo)? / Suwa Nobushige? [1] | siblings = Shigenao, Yorinari, Atsutada, Yorimori, Akishige, or Tsunemitsu? [1] | children = Tokitsugu, Takashige, Takatsugu, Banzai [1] | rename = Moritsugu [1] → Yorishige → Shoun (Dharma name) }}
'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[military commander]] active from the late [[Kamakura period]] to the [[Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).

revision.text:
{{Short description|Military commander}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Suwa Yorishige | era = Late Kamakura period - Nanbokucho period | birth = Unknown | death = August 19, 1335 (Kenmu 2) | alias = Moritaka? [2], Shoun Nyudo | rank = Junior Fifth Rank [2], Governor of Mikawa [1] | lord = Hojo Clan | clan = Suwa | parents = Father: Suwa Morishige? / Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo)? / Suwa Nobushige? [1] | siblings = Shigenao, Yorinari, Atsutada, Yorimori, Akishige, or Tsunemitsu? [1] | children = Tokitsugu, Takashige, Takatsugu, Banzai [1] | rename = Moritsugu [1] → Yorishige → Shoun (Dharma name) }}
'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[military commander]] active from the late [[Kamakura period]] to the [[Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige.png|A memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige (Miyagawa, [[Chino, Nagano|Chino City, Nagano Prefecture]]; the top of the tower shows signs of repair.) File:Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up).jpg|Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up) File:Suwa Shrine Maemiya Worship Site.jpg|Suwa Shrine Front Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho}} (The memorial tower is on the left as you descend) </gallery>
== Footnotes == <references/>
== Related works == * [[Matsui Yusei]] "[[The Elusive Samurai]]" (serialized in [[Weekly Shonen Jump|''Weekly Shonen Jump'']])
== Sources == * "Umematsuron" * "Taiheiki" (Volume 13, The Death of Tokiyuki in the Case of the Ashikaga Lord's Departure to the Eastern Provinces)

{{Short description|Military commander}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Suwa Yorishige | era = Late Kamakura period - Nanbokucho period | birth = Unknown | death = August 19, 1335 (Kenmu 2) | alias = Moritaka? [2], Shoun Nyudo | rank = Junior Fifth Rank [2], Governor of Mikawa [1] | lord = Hojo Clan | clan = Suwa | parents = Father: Suwa Morishige? / Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo)? / Suwa Nobushige? [1] | siblings = Shigenao, Yorinari, Atsutada, Yorimori, Akishige, or Tsunemitsu? [1] | children = Tokitsugu, Takashige, Takatsugu, Banzai [1] | rename = Moritsugu [1] → Yorishige → Shoun (Dharma name) }}
'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[military commander]] active from the late [[Kamakura period]] to the [[Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige.png|A memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige (Miyagawa, [[Chino, Nagano|Chino City, Nagano Prefecture]]; the top of the tower shows signs of repair.) File:Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up).jpg|Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up) File:Suwa Shrine Maemiya Worship Site.jpg|Suwa Shrine Front Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=draft}} (The memorial tower is on the left as you descend) </gallery>

== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).

{{Short description|Military commander}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Suwa Yorishige | era = Late Kamakura period - Nanbokucho period | birth = Unknown | death = August 19, 1335 (Kenmu 2) | alias = Moritaka? [2], Shoun Nyudo | Rank = Junior Fifth Rank [2], Governor of Mikawa [1] | lord = Hojo Clan | clan = Suwa | parents = Father: Suwa Morishige? / Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo)? / Suwa Nobushige? [1] | siblings = Shigenao, Yorinari, Atsutada, Yorimori, Akishige, or Tsunemitsu? [1] | children = Tokitsugu, Takashige, Takatsugu, Banzai [1] | rename = Moritsugu [1] → Yorishige → Shoun (Dharma name) }}
'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[military commander]] active from the late [[Kamakura period]] to the [[Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige.png|A memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige (Miyagawa, [[Chino, Nagano|Chino City, Nagano Prefecture]]; the top of the tower shows signs of repair.) {{Short description|Military commander}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Suwa Yorishige | era = Late Kamakura period - Nanbokucho period | birth = Unknown | death = August 19, 1335 (Kenmu 2) | alias = Moritaka? [2], Shoun Nyudo | Rank = Junior Fifth Rank [2], Governor of Mikawa [1] | lord = Hojo Clan | clan = Suwa | parents = Father: Suwa Morishige? / Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo)? / Suwa Nobushige? [1] | siblings = Shigenao, Yorinari, Atsutada, Yorimori, Akishige, or Tsunemitsu? [1] | children = Tokitsugu, Takashige, Takatsugu, Banzai [1] | rename = Moritsugu [1] → Yorishige → Shoun (Dharma name) }}
'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[military commander]] active from the late [[Kamakura period]] to the [[Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).

[1] | children = Tokitsugu, Takashige, Takatsugu, Banzai [1] | rename = Moritsugu [1] → Yorishige → Shoun (Dharma name) }}
'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[:en:Military commander|Military commander]] active from the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] to the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>

Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>

The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).

'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[:en:Military commander|Military commander]] active from the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] to the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)]][[id:Suwa Yorishige]][[ja:諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)]][[zh:諏訪賴重 (南北朝時代)]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|1335|9|6}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|native_name=諏訪頼重}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11631966]] Wikidata name: Suwa Yorishige [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Military commander}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Suwa Yorishige | era = Late Kamakura period - Nanbokucho period | birth = Unknown | death = August 19, 1335 (Kenmu 2) | alias = Moritaka? [2], Shoun Nyudo | Rank = Junior Fifth Rank [2], Governor of Mikawa [1] | lord = Hojo Clan | clan = Suwa | parents = Father: Suwa Morishige? / Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo)? / Suwa Nobushige? [1] | siblings = Shigenao, Yorinari, Atsutada, Yorimori, Akishige, or Tsunemitsu? [1] | children = Tokitsugu, Takashige, Takatsugu, Banzai [1] | rename = Moritsugu [1] → Yorishige → Shoun (Dharma name) }}
'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[:en:Military commander|Military commander]] active from the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] to the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige.png|A memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige (Miyagawa, [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino City, Nagano Prefecture]]; the top of the tower shows signs of repair.) File:Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up).jpg|Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up) File:Suwa Shrine Maemiya Worship Site.jpg|Suwa Shrine Front Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (The memorial tower is on the left as you descend) </gallery>
== Footnotes == <references/>
== Related works == * [[:en:Matsui Yusei|Matsui Yusei]] "[[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]]" (serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shonen Jump|''Weekly Shonen Jump'']])

[2], Shoun Nyudo | Rank = Junior Fifth Rank [2], Governor of Mikawa [1] | lord = Hojo Clan | clan = Suwa | parents = Father: Suwa Morishige? / Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo)? / Suwa Nobushige? [1] | siblings = Shigenao, Yorinari, Atsutada, Yorimori, Akishige, or Tsunemitsu? '''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[:en:Military commander|Military commander]] active from the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] to the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige.png|A memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige (Miyagawa, [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino City, Nagano Prefecture]]; the top of the tower shows signs of repair.) File:Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up).jpg|Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up) File:Suwa Shrine Maemiya Worship Site.jpg|Suwa Shrine Front Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (The memorial tower is on the left as you descend) </gallery>
== Footnotes == <references/>

[2], Shoun Nyudo | Rank = Junior Fifth Rank [2], Governor of Mikawa [1] | lord = Hojo Clan | clan = Suwa | parents = Father: Suwa Morishige? / Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo)? / Suwa Nobushige? '''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[:en:Military commander|Military commander]] active from the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] to the [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Northern and Southern Courts period]]. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hojo clan]], the ''shugo'' (governor) of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]], and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige.png|A memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige (Miyagawa, [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino City, Nagano Prefecture]]; the top of the tower shows signs of repair.) File:Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up).jpg|Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up) File:Suwa Shrine Maemiya Worship Site.jpg|Suwa Shrine Front Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (The memorial tower is on the left as you descend) </gallery>

[2], Shoun Nyudo | Rank = Junior Fifth Rank [2], Governor of Mikawa [1] | lord = Hojo Clan | clan = Suwa | parents = Father: Suwa Morishige? / Suwa Munetsune (Naoyuki Nyudo)? / Suwa Nobushige? [1] | children = Tokitsugu, Takashige, Takatsugu, Banzai [1] | rename = Moritsugu [1] → Yorishige → Shoun (Dharma name) }}
'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[:en:Military commander|Military commander]] active from the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Northern and Southern Courts period}}. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the {{ill|Hōjō clan|en|Hōjō clan|WD=Q834364|lt=Hojo clan}}, the ''shugo'' (governor) of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent {{ill|Hōjō Takatoki|en|Hōjō Takatoki|WD=Q2355334|lt=Hojo Takatoki}}, and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}} to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige.png|A memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige (Miyagawa, {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|WD=Q838660|lt=Chino City, Nagano Prefecture}}; the top of the tower shows signs of repair.) File:Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up).jpg|Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up) File:Suwa Shrine Maemiya Worship Site.jpg|Suwa Shrine Front Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (The memorial tower is on the left as you descend) </gallery>

'''Suwa Yorishige''' was a [[:en:Military commander|Military commander]] active from the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Northern and Southern Courts period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. He is said to have been the [[shrine priest|chief]] of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]], though details are uncertain. There are various theories regarding his parentage.
== Life == During the Kamakura period, the [[Suwa clan]] served as retainers to the {{ill|Hōjō clan|en|Hōjō clan|WD=Q834364|lt=Hojo clan|1=Hōjō clan}}, the ''shugo'' (governor) of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} for generations. During the Tosho-ji War, Suwa Tokimitsu (Enko Nyudo) of the Suwa clan committed suicide.
After the fall of the Kamakura Shogunate, the Suwa clan clashed with Ogasawara Sadamune, who was appointed as the new ''shugo'' of Shinano by the new-established [[Kenmu Restoration]]. Due to dissatisfaction with Ogasawara's rule, Yorishige and his son Suwa Tokitsugu supported [[Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the surviving son of Regent {{ill|Hōjō Takatoki|en|Hōjō Takatoki|WD=Q2355334|lt=Hojo Takatoki|1=Hōjō Takatoki}}, and initiated the Nakasendai Rebellion. With the help of the Miura clan and others, Yorishige defeated and killed Shibukawa Yoshiki, Iwamatsu Tsuneie, Imagawa Norimitsu (of the Nanboku-cho period), and Koyama Hidetomo. They temporarily occupied Kamakura and forced {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}} to flee, but were ultimately defeated by a punitive force led by Kiso Iemura, sent from Kyoto. Yorishige then committed suicide at Shochoju-in along with his son Tokitsugu and 43 others.<ref>Dai Nihon Shiryo, Volume 6, No. 2, p. 540</ref>
According to the genealogy in Volume 27 of the Suwa Shiryo Sosho, it is believed that the "Suwa Moritaka" who helped Hojo Tokiyuki escape from Kamakura and brought him to Shinano is the same person as Yorishige.<ref name="#2">[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1185905/7 "Suwa Historical Materials Collection" Volume 27]</ref>
The headship of the Suwa family (the position of ''Daishugi'') was passed down to his grandson, [[Suwa Yoritsugu|Yoritsugu]] (Tokitsugu's son).
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige.png|A memorial tower for Lord Suwa Teruun Yorishige (Miyagawa, {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|WD=Q838660|lt=Chino City, Nagano Prefecture|1=Chino, Nagano}}; the top of the tower shows signs of repair.) File:Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up).jpg|Torii gate of Suwa Taisha Shrine (signboard on the left, memorial tower on the right after climbing up) File:Suwa Shrine Maemiya Worship Site.jpg|Suwa Shrine Front Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (The memorial tower is on the left as you descend) </gallery>
== Footnotes == <references/>
